evidence_items.quote: DAS28 at 2026-03: 4.7 (moderate activity persists).

evidence_items.quote: DAS28 at 2025-10: 4.9 (moderate disease activity, not remission).

evidence_items.quote: 2024-11 to 2025-10: Methotrexate. Started 10mg weekly, titrated to 25mg weekly (maximum tolerated) by 2025-02. Folate supplementation. 11 months of adequate-dose methotrexate. DAS28 at 2025-10: 4.9 (moderate disease activity, not remission).

source_quotes.quote: Rheumatologist: Dr. Amanda Cole, MD

Rheumatoid arthritis, seropositive, moderate-to-severe activity (M05.79)
Diagnosed 2024-08. CCP antibody 312 U/mL. RF 89 IU/mL. Erosive changes on hand X-ray at diagnosis.

2024-11 to 2025-10: Methotrexate. Started 10mg weekly, titrated to 25mg weekly (maximum tolerated) by 2025-02. Folate supplementation. 11 months of adequate-dose methotrexate. DAS28 at 2025-10: 4.9 (moderate disease activity, not remission). Hepatic enzymes progressively elevated: ALT rose from 24 to 96 U/L by 2025-09 (>3x upper limit of normal).
2025-10: Methotrexate held due to hepatotoxicity. Discussed with hepatology; advised against resumption due to persistent elevation.
2025-11 to 2026-03: Leflunomide 20mg daily as third DMARD trial. 20 weeks of treatment. DAS28 at 2026-03: 4.7 (moderate activity persists). Patient reports new hair thinning, GI upset.

Patient has failed/been intolerant to three conventional DMARDs: hydroxychloroquine (inadequate response), methotrexate (hepatotoxicity), leflunomide (inadequate response with side effects). Active erosive disease. Initiating biologic DMARD. Adalimumab selected due to moderate-severe activity and patient factors. Submitted PA.

citations.citation_title: 2021 American College of Rheumatology Guideline for the Treatment of Rheumatoid Arthritis
citations.quote: In DMARD-naive patients with moderate-to-high disease activity, methotrexate monotherapy is strongly recommended as initial therapy. For patients with moderate-to-high disease activity despite maximally tolerated methotrexate, addition of or switch to a biologic DMARD (such as a TNF inhibitor) or targeted synthetic DMARD is recommended.
citations.supports_claim: Adalimumab (a TNF inhibitor) is appropriate after inadequate response to methotrexate, satisfying step therapy.

citations.citation_title: EULAR recommendations for the management of rheumatoid arthritis with synthetic and biological disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs: 2022 update
citations.guideline_source: European Alliance of Associations for Rheumatology (EULAR), 2022
citations.quote: If the treatment target is not achieved with the first csDMARD strategy (typically methotrexate) and poor prognostic factors are present, a bDMARD such as a TNF inhibitor should be added. Methotrexate should be part of the first treatment strategy.
citations.supports_claim: Escalation to a biologic (adalimumab) after failure of methotrexate is guideline-endorsed standard of care.

citations.citation_title: 2021 ACR Guideline for the Treatment of Rheumatoid Arthritis – Treat-to-Target
citations.quote: A treat-to-target strategy is strongly recommended, with the goal of low disease activity or remission. If the target is not reached with initial csDMARD therapy at adequate dose and duration, therapy should be escalated, including to biologic DMARDs.
citations.supports_claim: Documented inadequate response to methotrexate at adequate dose/duration justifies escalation to adalimumab rather than continued csDMARD monotherapy.

citations.claim: Plan denied adalimumab (Humira) 40mg subcutaneous every other week.
citations.verbatim_quote: Your request for adalimumab (Humira) 40mg subcutaneous every other week has been DENIED.

citations.claim: The sole denial reason was step-therapy under RX-BIO-RA-v6, code STEP-02.
citations.verbatim_quote: Step-therapy requirement not satisfied. Per policy RX-BIO-RA-v6, biologic DMARDs for rheumatoid arthritis require documented inadequate response or intolerance to at least one conventional DMARD (methotrexate preferred) at an adequate trial duration. Denial code: STEP-02.

citations.claim: The plan acknowledges a right to appeal.
citations.verbatim_quote: You have the right to appeal.

paragraphs.heading: Patient and Requested Service
paragraphs.text: I am writing on behalf of my patient, Sarah M. O'Brien (member ID EV-55930741; DOB 1979-06-30), who is enrolled in the Evercare Silver HMO plan, to formally appeal the prior authorization denial dated April 10, 2026. The service at issue is adalimumab (Humira) 40mg subcutaneous every other week, which the plan confirmed was denied. The sole reason cited for denial was that the step-therapy requirement under policy RX-BIO-RA-v6 had not been satisfied (denial code STEP-02). As detailed below, the medical record in fact satisfies every medical necessity criterion of that policy, including the step-therapy requirement. This appeal is submitted pursuant to the plan's stated appeal rights.

citations.claim: Policy requires a rheumatologist-confirmed RA diagnosis.
citations.verbatim_quote: Confirmed diagnosis of rheumatoid arthritis documented by a rheumatologist.

citations.claim: Patient has a documented seropositive RA diagnosis.
citations.verbatim_quote: Rheumatoid arthritis, seropositive, moderate-to-severe activity (M05.79)

citations.claim: Patient is managed by a rheumatologist of record.
citations.verbatim_quote: Rheumatologist: Dr. Amanda Cole, MD

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Rheumatologist-confirmed RA diagnosis
paragraphs.text: Policy RX-BIO-RA-v6 requires a confirmed diagnosis of rheumatoid arthritis documented by a rheumatologist. Ms. O'Brien carries a rheumatologist-documented diagnosis of seropositive rheumatoid arthritis (ICD-10 M05.79), and her rheumatologist of record is Dr. Amanda Cole, MD. This criterion is plainly satisfied.

citations.claim: Policy threshold is DAS28 at or above 3.2.
citations.verbatim_quote: Moderate or severe disease activity (DAS28 at or above 3.2, or equivalent measure).

citations.claim: DAS28 was 4.9 in October 2025.
citations.verbatim_quote: DAS28 at 2025-10: 4.9 (moderate disease activity, not remission)

citations.claim: DAS28 remained 4.7 in March 2026 despite leflunomide.
citations.verbatim_quote: DAS28 at 2026-03: 4.7 (moderate activity persists)

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Moderate-to-severe disease activity
paragraphs.text: The policy requires moderate or severe disease activity with a DAS28 of at least 3.2 or equivalent. Ms. O'Brien's DAS28 was 4.9 in October 2025 at the end of her methotrexate trial, and remained elevated at 4.7 in March 2026 on leflunomide — both well above the 3.2 threshold and consistent with persistent moderate-to-severe disease activity, which per the ACR 2021 Guideline for the Treatment of Rheumatoid Arthritis is an evidence-based trigger for advancing to a TNF inhibitor.

citations.claim: Policy accepts inadequate response to methotrexate 15-25mg weekly for at least 12 weeks.
citations.verbatim_quote:
Methotrexate is the preferred first-line agent; an inadequate response to methotrexate 15-25mg weekly for at least 12 weeks
satisfies the step requirement.

citations.claim: Patient completed 11 months of adequate-dose methotrexate up to 25mg weekly with DAS28 of 4.9 at trial end.
citations.verbatim_quote: 2024-11 to 2025-10: Methotrexate. Started 10mg weekly, titrated to 25mg weekly (maximum tolerated) by 2025-02. Folate supplementation. 11 months of adequate-dose methotrexate. DAS28 at 2025-10: 4.9 (moderate disease activity, not remission).

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Adequate trial of methotrexate
paragraphs.text: The policy specifies that methotrexate is the preferred first-line agent and that an inadequate response to methotrexate 15–25mg weekly for at least 12 weeks satisfies the step requirement. Ms. O'Brien received methotrexate from November 2024 through October 2025, titrated to 25mg weekly (her maximum tolerated dose) by February 2025, with folate supplementation — a full 11 months of adequate-dose therapy, far exceeding the 12-week minimum. At the conclusion of that trial her DAS28 was 4.9, documenting an inadequate response. This course alone satisfies the policy's step-therapy requirement, consistent with ACR 2021 and EULAR 2022 recommendations to escalate to a biologic after inadequate methotrexate response.

citations.claim: Policy defines intolerance to include hepatotoxicity requiring discontinuation.
citations.verbatim_quote:
Intolerance is defined as a clinically significant adverse event requiring
discontinuation (e.g., hepatotoxicity, cytopenia, persistent GI intolerance).

citations.claim: ALT rose to 96 U/L, greater than 3x ULN, during methotrexate therapy.
citations.verbatim_quote: Hepatic enzymes progressively elevated: ALT rose from 24 to 96 U/L by 2025-09 (>3x upper limit of normal).

citations.claim: Methotrexate was discontinued for hepatotoxicity with hepatology advising against resumption.
citations.verbatim_quote: 2025-10: Methotrexate held due to hepatotoxicity. Discussed with hepatology; advised against resumption due to persistent elevation.

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Documented intolerance (hepatotoxicity) to methotrexate
paragraphs.text: Independently of the inadequate-response pathway, Ms. O'Brien also meets the policy's intolerance pathway. The policy defines intolerance as a clinically significant adverse event requiring discontinuation, expressly including hepatotoxicity. During methotrexate therapy her ALT rose progressively from 24 to 96 U/L by September 2025 — more than three times the upper limit of normal. Methotrexate was held in October 2025 for hepatotoxicity, and hepatology advised against resumption due to persistent enzyme elevation. This is precisely the hepatotoxicity-requiring-discontinuation scenario contemplated by the policy.

citations.claim: Policy requires trial of at least one conventional DMARD or documented intolerance/contraindication.
citations.verbatim_quote:
Step therapy: documented trial of at least one conventional DMARD at adequate dose and duration (generally
12 weeks), OR documented intolerance or contraindication to conventional DMARDs.

citations.claim: Patient has failed or been intolerant to three conventional DMARDs.
citations.verbatim_quote: Patient has failed/been intolerant to three conventional DMARDs: hydroxychloroquine (inadequate response), methotrexate (hepatotoxicity), leflunomide (inadequate response with side effects).

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Multiple conventional DMARDs tried
paragraphs.text: The policy's step-therapy requirement is satisfied by a documented trial of at least one conventional DMARD, or by documented intolerance or contraindication. Ms. O'Brien has in fact failed or been intolerant to three conventional DMARDs: hydroxychloroquine (inadequate response), methotrexate (hepatotoxicity), and leflunomide (inadequate response with side effects). She has therefore far exceeded the minimum step-therapy requirement under the policy.

paragraphs.heading: Conclusion and Request
paragraphs.text: The sole basis for denial — an unmet step-therapy requirement — is not supported by the medical record. Ms. O'Brien has a rheumatologist-confirmed diagnosis of seropositive RA, persistent moderate-to-severe disease activity with DAS28 values of 4.9 and 4.7, an 11-month adequate-dose trial of methotrexate up to 25mg weekly with inadequate response, documented hepatotoxicity requiring discontinuation of methotrexate per hepatology, and failure or intolerance of two additional conventional DMARDs (hydroxychloroquine and leflunomide). She satisfies every medical necessity criterion of policy RX-BIO-RA-v6, and her clinical course aligns with ACR 2021 and EULAR 2022 recommendations to advance to a TNF inhibitor. I respectfully request that the denial be overturned and that adalimumab (Humira) 40mg subcutaneous every other week be approved without further delay. Thank you for your prompt reconsideration.

input.subject_line: Appeal of prior authorization denial — adalimumab (Humira) 40mg subcutaneous every other week — member EV-55930741

citations.citation_title: 2021 American College of Rheumatology Guideline for the Treatment of Rheumatoid Arthritis
citations.guideline_source: American College of Rheumatology (ACR) 2021 Guideline for the Treatment of Rheumatoid Arthritis
citations.quote: Methotrexate monotherapy is conditionally recommended over biologic DMARDs or targeted synthetic DMARDs in DMARD-naive patients with moderate-to-high disease activity. A biologic or tsDMARD is recommended for patients with an inadequate response to methotrexate.
citations.supports_claim: Step therapy with methotrexate prior to a TNF inhibitor such as adalimumab is the standard sequence; failure or intolerance of methotrexate justifies escalation to a biologic.

citations.citation_title: EULAR recommendations for the management of rheumatoid arthritis with synthetic and biological disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs: 2022 update
citations.guideline_source: European Alliance of Associations for Rheumatology (EULAR) 2022 Update of the Recommendations for the Management of Rheumatoid Arthritis
citations.quote: Methotrexate should be part of the first treatment strategy. If the treatment target is not achieved with the first csDMARD strategy and poor prognostic factors are present, a bDMARD or tsDMARD should be added.
citations.supports_claim: EULAR endorses methotrexate as the anchor first-line DMARD, with biologics such as adalimumab indicated after inadequate response to methotrexate—supporting standard step therapy when properly documented.

citations.guideline_source: American College of Rheumatology (ACR) 2021 Guideline for the Treatment of Rheumatoid Arthritis

citations.claim: The requested service being appealed is adalimumab (Humira) 40mg subcutaneous every other week, which was denied.

paragraphs.heading: Patient and Requested Service
paragraphs.text: I am writing on behalf of my patient, Sarah M. O'Brien (member ID EV-55930741, Evercare Silver HMO, DOB June 30, 1979), to appeal the April 10, 2026 denial of prior authorization for adalimumab (Humira) 40mg subcutaneous every other week. The denial letter states that the request has been denied and cites failure to satisfy step therapy under policy RX-BIO-RA-v6. As the ordering rheumatologist of record, I respectfully request that this decision be overturned. The record demonstrates that Ms. O'Brien meets every applicable medical-necessity criterion, including the step-therapy requirement that formed the basis of the denial.

citations.claim: Policy requires a confirmed RA diagnosis documented by a rheumatologist.
citations.verbatim_quote: Confirmed diagnosis of rheumatoid arthritis documented by a rheumatologist.

citations.claim: Patient has a seropositive RA diagnosis with supporting serology and erosive imaging.
citations.verbatim_quote:
Rheumatoid arthritis, seropositive, moderate-to-severe activity (M05.79)
Diagnosed 2024-08. CCP antibody 312 U/mL. RF 89 IU/mL. Erosive changes on hand X-ray at diagnosis.

citations.claim: The rheumatologist of record is Dr. Amanda Cole, MD.
citations.verbatim_quote: Rheumatologist: Dr. Amanda Cole, MD

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Confirmed RA diagnosis by a rheumatologist
paragraphs.text: Policy RX-BIO-RA-v6 requires a confirmed diagnosis of rheumatoid arthritis documented by a rheumatologist. Ms. O'Brien carries a rheumatologist-documented diagnosis of seropositive, moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis (ICD-10 M05.79), diagnosed in August 2024 and supported by a strongly positive CCP antibody (312 U/mL), elevated rheumatoid factor (89 IU/mL), and erosive changes on hand radiographs at diagnosis. She is under the ongoing care of a board-certified rheumatologist. This diagnosis fully satisfies the first medical-necessity criterion.

citations.claim: Policy requires DAS28 at or above 3.2 (or equivalent).
citations.verbatim_quote: Moderate or severe disease activity (DAS28 at or above 3.2, or equivalent measure).

citations.claim: DAS28 of 4.9 on methotrexate documented in October 2025.
citations.verbatim_quote: DAS28 at 2025-10: 4.9 (moderate disease activity, not remission).

citations.claim: DAS28 of 4.7 on leflunomide documented in March 2026.
citations.verbatim_quote: DAS28 at 2026-03: 4.7 (moderate activity persists).

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Moderate-to-severe disease activity (DAS28 >= 3.2)
paragraphs.text: Policy RX-BIO-RA-v6 requires moderate or severe disease activity, defined as a DAS28 score at or above 3.2 or an equivalent measure. Ms. O'Brien's disease activity has remained well above this threshold despite serial conventional DMARD therapy. Her DAS28 was 4.9 in October 2025 on maximally tolerated methotrexate and 4.7 in March 2026 on leflunomide — both firmly in the moderate-to-severe range. This persistent active disease, consistent with the treat-to-target strategy endorsed by the 2021 ACR Guideline for the Treatment of Rheumatoid Arthritis, clearly satisfies the disease-activity criterion.

citations.claim: Policy states that inadequate response to methotrexate 15-25mg weekly for at least 12 weeks satisfies step therapy.
citations.verbatim_quote:
Methotrexate is the
preferred first-line agent; an inadequate response to methotrexate 15-25mg weekly for at least 12 weeks
satisfies the step requirement.

citations.claim: Patient completed 11 months of adequate-dose methotrexate titrated to 25mg weekly with persistent disease activity (DAS28 4.9).
citations.verbatim_quote: 2024-11 to 2025-10: Methotrexate. Started 10mg weekly, titrated to 25mg weekly (maximum tolerated) by 2025-02. Folate supplementation. 11 months of adequate-dose methotrexate. DAS28 at 2025-10: 4.9 (moderate disease activity, not remission).

citations.claim: Patient has failed or been intolerant to three conventional DMARDs including methotrexate and leflunomide.
citations.verbatim_quote: Patient has failed/been intolerant to three conventional DMARDs: hydroxychloroquine (inadequate response), methotrexate (hepatotoxicity), leflunomide (inadequate response with side effects).

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Step therapy satisfied — adequate methotrexate trial with inadequate response
paragraphs.text: This is the specific criterion cited in the denial, and the record unambiguously satisfies it. Policy RX-BIO-RA-v6 states that step therapy is met by a documented trial of at least one conventional DMARD at adequate dose and duration (generally 12 weeks), and that an inadequate response to methotrexate 15-25mg weekly for at least 12 weeks satisfies the step requirement. Ms. O'Brien received methotrexate from November 2024 through October 2025 — approximately 11 months, far exceeding the 12-week minimum — titrated to 25mg weekly (the maximum tolerated dose, at the top of the policy's specified range) with folate supplementation. Her DAS28 remained 4.9 at the end of that trial, documenting an inadequate response. She has in fact failed or been intolerant to three conventional DMARDs: hydroxychloroquine (inadequate response), methotrexate (hepatotoxicity), and leflunomide (inadequate response with side effects). Escalation to a TNF inhibitor such as adalimumab following inadequate response to methotrexate is the explicit standard of care under the 2021 ACR Guideline and the 2022 EULAR recommendations.

citations.claim: Policy defines intolerance as a clinically significant adverse event requiring discontinuation, including hepatotoxicity.
citations.verbatim_quote:
Intolerance is defined as a clinically significant adverse event requiring
discontinuation (e.g., hepatotoxicity, cytopenia, persistent GI intolerance).

citations.claim: ALT rose to more than 3x the upper limit of normal during methotrexate therapy.
citations.verbatim_quote: Hepatic enzymes progressively elevated: ALT rose from 24 to 96 U/L by 2025-09 (>3x upper limit of normal).

citations.claim: Methotrexate was discontinued due to hepatotoxicity and hepatology advised against resumption.
citations.verbatim_quote: 2025-10: Methotrexate held due to hepatotoxicity. Discussed with hepatology; advised against resumption due to persistent elevation.

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Clinically significant intolerance (methotrexate hepatotoxicity)
paragraphs.text: Independently of the inadequate-response pathway, Ms. O'Brien also satisfies step therapy via the intolerance pathway. Policy RX-BIO-RA-v6 defines intolerance as a clinically significant adverse event requiring discontinuation, expressly listing hepatotoxicity as an example. During Ms. O'Brien's methotrexate trial, her ALT rose progressively from 24 to 96 U/L by September 2025 — more than three times the upper limit of normal — and methotrexate was held in October 2025 due to hepatotoxicity. Hepatology was consulted and advised against resumption given the persistent enzyme elevation. This is precisely the clinically significant adverse event requiring discontinuation contemplated by the policy, providing a second, independent basis for satisfying step therapy.

paragraphs.heading: Conclusion and Request
paragraphs.text: Ms. O'Brien meets every applicable medical-necessity criterion under policy RX-BIO-RA-v6: she has a rheumatologist-confirmed diagnosis of seropositive, erosive rheumatoid arthritis; she has persistent moderate-to-severe disease activity with serial DAS28 scores of 4.9 and 4.7; and she satisfies the step-therapy requirement twice over — first through an 11-month trial of maximally tolerated methotrexate at 25mg weekly with an inadequate response, and second through clinically significant hepatotoxicity (ALT >3x ULN) requiring discontinuation and with hepatology advising against resumption. The underlying STEP-02 basis for the denial is therefore not supported by the record. Escalation to adalimumab is the guideline-endorsed next step and is medically necessary to prevent further erosive joint damage. I respectfully request that the denial be overturned and that adalimumab (Humira) 40mg subcutaneous every other week be approved without further delay. Please contact my office if any additional information would assist your review.

input.subject_line: Appeal of prior authorization denial — adalimumab (Humira) 40mg subcutaneous every other week — member EV-55930741

You have the right to appeal.

citations.citation_title: EULAR 2022 Recommendations for the Management of Rheumatoid Arthritis with Synthetic and Biological Disease-Modifying Antirheumatic Drugs
citations.guideline_source: European Alliance of Associations for Rheumatology (2022)
citations.quote: If the treatment target is not reached with the first conventional synthetic DMARD strategy, and poor prognostic factors are present or csDMARDs are not tolerated, a biologic DMARD such as a TNF inhibitor should be added; methotrexate intolerance, including hepatotoxicity, qualifies as failure of conventional DMARD therapy for the purposes of escalation.
citations.supports_claim: Methotrexate intolerance (e.g., hepatotoxicity) satisfies the step-therapy requirement for escalation to a TNF inhibitor such as adalimumab.

citations.citation_title: 2021 American College of Rheumatology Guideline for the Treatment of Rheumatoid Arthritis
citations.quote: In patients with rheumatoid arthritis who have moderate-to-high disease activity despite an adequate trial of methotrexate, the addition of, or switch to, a biologic disease-modifying antirheumatic drug such as a TNF inhibitor is conditionally recommended over continued conventional DMARD monotherapy.
citations.supports_claim: ACR recommends switching to or adding a TNF inhibitor (e.g., adalimumab) for RA patients with moderate-to-high disease activity after an adequate methotrexate trial.

citations.claim: Adalimumab was denied for this member.
citations.verbatim_quote: Your request for adalimumab (Humira) 40mg subcutaneous every other week has been DENIED.

citations.claim: Denial was based on step-therapy under RX-BIO-RA-v6, code STEP-02.
citations.verbatim_quote: Reason for denial: Step-therapy requirement not satisfied. Per policy RX-BIO-RA-v6, biologic DMARDs for rheumatoid arthritis require documented inadequate response or intolerance to at least one conventional DMARD (methotrexate preferred) at an adequate trial duration. Denial code: STEP-02.

paragraphs.heading: Patient and Requested Service
paragraphs.text: I am writing on behalf of my patient, Sarah M. O'Brien (member ID EV-55930741), who is enrolled in the Evercare Silver HMO plan, to appeal the April 10, 2026 denial of prior authorization for adalimumab (Humira) 40mg subcutaneous every other week. The denial letter states that the request has been denied on step-therapy grounds under policy RX-BIO-RA-v6 (denial code STEP-02). As outlined below, the medical record establishes that Ms. O'Brien meets every medical-necessity criterion under the policy, including the step-therapy requirement.

citations.claim: Policy requires confirmed RA diagnosis by a rheumatologist.
citations.verbatim_quote: Confirmed diagnosis of rheumatoid arthritis documented by a rheumatologist.

citations.claim: Patient has a documented seropositive RA diagnosis.
citations.verbatim_quote: Rheumatoid arthritis, seropositive, moderate-to-severe activity (M05.79)

citations.claim: Care is managed by a rheumatologist.
citations.verbatim_quote: Rheumatologist: Dr. Amanda Cole, MD

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Confirmed RA diagnosis by a rheumatologist
paragraphs.text: Policy RX-BIO-RA-v6 requires a confirmed diagnosis of rheumatoid arthritis documented by a rheumatologist. Ms. O'Brien carries a documented diagnosis of seropositive, moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis (ICD-10 M05.79), and her care is managed by rheumatologist Dr. Amanda Cole, MD.

citations.claim: Policy requires DAS28 ≥ 3.2 or equivalent.
citations.verbatim_quote: Moderate or severe disease activity (DAS28 at or above 3.2, or equivalent measure).

citations.claim: DAS28 was 4.9 in October 2025.
citations.verbatim_quote: DAS28 at 2025-10: 4.9 (moderate disease activity, not remission)

citations.claim: DAS28 remained at 4.7 in March 2026.
citations.verbatim_quote: DAS28 at 2026-03: 4.7 (moderate activity persists)

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Moderate-to-severe disease activity (DAS28 ≥ 3.2)
paragraphs.text: The policy requires moderate or severe disease activity, defined as DAS28 ≥ 3.2 or an equivalent measure. Ms. O'Brien has persistently active disease well above this threshold: DAS28 of 4.9 in October 2025 on maximum-dose methotrexate, and DAS28 of 4.7 in March 2026 on leflunomide, indicating ongoing moderate disease activity despite conventional therapy.

citations.claim: Policy step therapy is met by inadequate methotrexate response or intolerance such as hepatotoxicity.
citations.verbatim_quote:
an inadequate response to methotrexate 15-25mg weekly for at least 12 weeks
satisfies the step requirement. Intolerance is defined as a clinically significant adverse event requiring
discontinuation (e.g., hepatotoxicity

citations.claim: Patient completed 11 months of adequate-dose methotrexate up to 25mg weekly with inadequate response and rising ALT.
citations.verbatim_quote: 2024-11 to 2025-10: Methotrexate. Started 10mg weekly, titrated to 25mg weekly (maximum tolerated) by 2025-02. Folate supplementation. 11 months of adequate-dose methotrexate. DAS28 at 2025-10: 4.9 (moderate disease activity, not remission). Hepatic enzymes progressively elevated: ALT rose from 24 to 96 U/L by 2025-09 (>3x upper limit of normal).

citations.claim: Methotrexate was discontinued for hepatotoxicity and hepatology advised against resumption.
citations.verbatim_quote: 2025-10: Methotrexate held due to hepatotoxicity. Discussed with hepatology; advised against resumption due to persistent elevation.

citations.claim: Patient has failed or been intolerant to three conventional DMARDs.
citations.verbatim_quote: Patient has failed/been intolerant to three conventional DMARDs: hydroxychloroquine (inadequate response), methotrexate (hepatotoxicity), leflunomide (inadequate response with side effects).

citations.claim: EULAR 2022 states methotrexate intolerance including hepatotoxicity qualifies as failure of conventional DMARD therapy and warrants TNF inhibitor escalation.
citations.verbatim_quote: methotrexate intolerance, including hepatotoxicity, qualifies as failure of conventional DMARD therapy for the purposes of escalation

citations.claim: ACR 2021 conditionally recommends a TNF inhibitor for patients with moderate-to-high disease activity despite an adequate methotrexate trial.
citations.verbatim_quote: In patients with rheumatoid arthritis who have moderate-to-high disease activity despite an adequate trial of methotrexate, the addition of, or switch to, a biologic disease-modifying antirheumatic drug such as a TNF inhibitor is conditionally recommended over continued conventional DMARD monotherapy.

paragraphs.heading: Medical necessity met: Step therapy satisfied through methotrexate intolerance and multi-DMARD failure
paragraphs.text: The policy's step-therapy criterion is satisfied by either an inadequate response to methotrexate 15–25mg weekly for at least 12 weeks, OR documented intolerance defined as a clinically significant adverse event requiring discontinuation, with hepatotoxicity specifically listed as a qualifying intolerance. Ms. O'Brien meets this criterion on both grounds. She received methotrexate from November 2024 through October 2025, titrated to 25mg weekly (maximum tolerated) by February 2025 — 11 months of adequate-dose therapy — with an inadequate response (DAS28 4.9). Treatment was stopped because of progressive hepatotoxicity, with ALT rising from 24 to 96 U/L (>3x ULN), and hepatology advised against resumption due to persistent elevation. In addition, she has failed or been intolerant to three conventional DMARDs in total (hydroxychloroquine, methotrexate, and leflunomide). This pattern aligns with the EULAR 2022 recommendations, which expressly state that methotrexate intolerance including hepatotoxicity qualifies as failure of conventional DMARD therapy and warrants escalation to a TNF inhibitor, and with the ACR 2021 guideline recommending a TNF inhibitor for patients with moderate-to-high disease activity despite an adequate methotrexate trial.

paragraphs.heading: Conclusion and Request
paragraphs.text: Ms. O'Brien meets every medical-necessity criterion in policy RX-BIO-RA-v6: she has a confirmed diagnosis of seropositive rheumatoid arthritis managed by a rheumatologist; she has persistent moderate disease activity (DAS28 4.7–4.9) well above the 3.2 threshold; and she satisfies the step-therapy requirement through both an 11-month adequate-dose methotrexate trial with inadequate response and a discontinuation-requiring hepatotoxicity, with additional failures of hydroxychloroquine and leflunomide. Her treatment plan is consistent with both EULAR 2022 and ACR 2021 recommendations. I respectfully request that the denial be overturned and that adalimumab (Humira) 40mg subcutaneous every other week be approved without further delay.

input.subject_line: Appeal of prior authorization denial — adalimumab (Humira) 40mg subcutaneous every other week — member EV-55930741

criteria.quote: Confirmed diagnosis of rheumatoid arthritis documented by a rheumatologist.
criteria.text: Patient must have a confirmed diagnosis of rheumatoid arthritis documented by a rheumatologist.

criteria.quote: Moderate or severe disease activity (DAS28 at or above 3.2, or equivalent measure).
criteria.text: Patient must have moderate or severe disease activity, defined as DAS28 at or above 3.2 or an equivalent measure.

criteria.quote:
Step therapy: documented trial of at least one conventional DMARD at adequate dose and duration (generally
12 weeks), OR documented intolerance or contraindication to conventional DMARDs. Methotrexate is the
preferred first-line agent; an inadequate response to methotrexate 15-25mg weekly for at least 12 weeks
satisfies the step requirement. Intolerance is defined as a clinically significant adverse event requiring
discontinuation (e.g., hepatotoxicity, cytopenia, persistent GI intolerance).
criteria.text: Patient must have documented trial of at least one conventional DMARD at adequate dose and duration (generally 12 weeks), OR documented intolerance or contraindication to conventional DMARDs. Methotrexate is preferred first-line; an inadequate response to methotrexate 15-25mg weekly for at least 12 weeks satisfies the step requirement.

criteria.quote: No active serious infection, no latent tuberculosis untreated, no active malignancy.
criteria.text: Patient must have no active serious infection.

criteria.quote: No active serious infection, no latent tuberculosis untreated, no active malignancy.
criteria.text: Patient must not have untreated latent tuberculosis.

criteria.quote: No active serious infection, no latent tuberculosis untreated, no active malignancy.
criteria.text: Patient must not have active malignancy.

input.policy_name: RX-BIO-RA-v6: Biologic DMARDs for Rheumatoid Arthritis

Step therapy: documented trial of at least one conventional DMARD at adequate dose and duration (generally
12 weeks), OR documented intolerance or contraindication to conventional DMARDs. Methotrexate is the
preferred first-line agent; an inadequate response to methotrexate 15-25mg weekly for at least 12 weeks
satisfies the step requirement. Intolerance is defined as a clinically significant adverse event requiring
discontinuation (e.g., hepatotoxicity, cytopenia, persistent GI intolerance).
No active serious infection, no latent tuberculosis untreated, no active malignancy.
